title: Salty Chewy Moroccan Quinoa Tagine
description: A savory, fluffy tagine packed with aromatic spices, tender quinoa, and a punch of salty olives and preserved lemon, perfect for meal prep and easy egg‑free dining.

ingredients.name: Spices mix
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp cinnamon, 1/4 tsp cayenne

ingredients.name: Seasoning
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp fresh cilantro, 1 tbsp fresh parsley

instructions: Rinse quinoa under running water until the water runs clear; drain well.

In a medium saucepan, combine quinoa and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until quinoa is tender and liquid absorbed, about 15 minutes. Remove from heat, fluff with a fork, and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add diced onion and carrot; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add diced zucchini, minced garlic, and the spice mix. Cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Stir in chickpeas, preserved lemon zest and slices, and sliced olives. Cook for another 3 minutes to meld flavors.
Add the cooked quinoa to the skillet. Pour in lemon juice and the remaining tablespoon of olive oil. Toss to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes to allow the tagine flavors to infuse.
Remove from heat and stir in fresh cilantro and parsley. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, optionally with crusty bread or a side of dairy‑free yogurt for dipping.
